title: City Walk Outcomes – March 2026
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: April 22, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: City Walk Outcomes – March 2026
The City Walks Program is designed to enhance quality of life for residents, neighborhoods, and commercial areas, while expediting service delivery to vulnerable populations.

Coordinated by Clean and Safe, City Walks are data-driven and strategically aligned with broader organizational initiatives. The City Walks Program uses an interdepartmental approach to promote proactive problem-solving, address issues early, collaborate with community members, and coordinate with departmental initiatives to connect residents, businesses, services, and enforcement efforts. City Walks take place four days per week, Monday to Thursday, including two days dedicated to City Walks within the Business Improvement District (BID) in partnership with the Downtown Reno Partnership.

City Walks Departments
• Clean and Safe – Community Engagement and Services
• Code Enforcement – Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Park and River Rangers – Parks and Recreation (Based on locations)
• Community Action & Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), and Parking Enforcement – Reno Police

The following is an overview of City Walk operations and outputs for March 2026:

March 2026 Highlights
• 17 city walks
• 94 community member contacts
• 19 yards of trash collected
• 11 occupied vehicles posted
• 9 citations
• 4 Code Enforcement cases created
• 3 abandoned vehicles posted
• 5 city walks in RPD DEEDS zone

Monday, March 2
• Locations: Along Delucchi Lane from Virginia Street to Airway Drive
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe
o Code Enforcement
o CAO and Parking Enforcement
• Outcomes:
o 2 individuals contacted
▪ 1 individual accessed REMSA services
▪ 1 individual declined services
o 2 nuisance vehicles issued 24-hour notice postings
o 3 abandoned vehicles issued 72-hour notice postings

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: This memo reports on City Walk operations for March 2026, covering 17 walks across Reno neighborhoods and the Business Improvement District. Teams from multiple departments contacted 94 community members, collected 19 yards of trash, posted 11 occupied and 3 abandoned vehicles, issued 9 citations, and opened 4 Code Enforcement cases. Five walks took place within the RPD DEEDS zone. The program uses an interdepartmental approach to connect vulnerable residents with services and address quality-of-life concerns proactively.
fields.key_stats: 17 city walks conducted; 94 community member contacts; 19 yards of trash collected; 11 occupied vehicles posted (24-hour notice); 3 abandoned vehicles posted (72-hour notice); 9 citations issued; 4 Code Enforcement cases created; 5 walks in RPD DEEDS zone; 1 arrest for a warrant

people: Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
